title: Get All Processes
description: Implement get all processes use case to allow reads of many items

title: Implement GetProcessesQuery and Handler
description:
Create GetProcessesQuery record with filtering, sorting, and pagination parameters. Implement GetProcessesQueryHandler following CQRS pattern with:

- Query parameters: SearchTerm (string?), ConnectionFilter (enum: Any/WithConnections/WithoutConnections), SortBy (Name/CreatedAt), SortDirection (ASC/DESC), Page (int), Limit (int)
- Return PagedResult<ProcessResponse> with total count and items
- Use PostgreSQL pg_trgm for fuzzy search on process name
- Implement dynamic WHERE clause building for filters
- Add ORDER BY for sorting by name or creation date
- Include OFFSET/LIMIT for pagination

Files to create/modify:
- src/backend/ProcessGraph.Application/Queries/GetProcessesQuery.cs
- src/backend/ProcessGraph.Application/Queries/GetProcessesQueryHandler.cs
- src/backend/ProcessGraph.Application/Models/PagedResult.cs (if needed)

title: Extend IProcessRepository with GetAllAsync method
description:
Implement the GetAllAsync method in IProcessRepository interface and ProcessRepository class to support filtering, sorting, and pagination:

- Update IProcessRepository interface with GetAllAsync overload that accepts query parameters
- Implement in ProcessRepository.cs using raw SQL via Dapper
- Use PostgreSQL ILIKE with % wildcard for fuzzy search on name
- Implement pg_trgm similarity search for better fuzzy matching
- Add dynamic WHERE clause construction for connection filtering
- Support sorting by name (ASC/DESC) and created_at (ASC/DESC)
- Include total count query for pagination metadata
- Add OFFSET/LIMIT for pagination

Connection filtering logic:
- WithConnections: processes where graph has nodes with connections
- WithoutConnections: processes where graph has no node connections
- Any: no filtering applied

Files to modify:
- src/backend/ProcessGraph.Domain/Repositories/IProcessRepository.cs
- src/backend/ProcessGraph.Infrastructure/Repositories/ProcessRepository.cs

title: Create ProcessConnectionFilter enum and related models
description:
Create supporting models for the GetProcesses functionality:

1. ProcessConnectionFilter enum:
   - Any: No connection filtering
   - WithConnections: Only processes with node connections
   - WithoutConnections: Only processes without node connections

2. ProcessSortBy enum:
   - Name: Sort by process name
   - CreatedAt: Sort by creation date

3. SortDirection enum (if not exists):
   - Ascending: ASC order
   - Descending: DESC order

4. PagedResult<T> class (if not exists):
   - Items: List of results
   - TotalCount: Total number of items
   - Page: Current page number
   - PageSize: Items per page
   - TotalPages: Calculated total pages

Files to create/modify:
- src/backend/ProcessGraph.Domain/Enums/ProcessConnectionFilter.cs
- src/backend/ProcessGraph.Domain/Enums/ProcessSortBy.cs
- src/backend/ProcessGraph.Domain/Enums/SortDirection.cs (if needed)
- src/backend/ProcessGraph.Application/Models/PagedResult.cs (if needed)

title: Update ProcessEndpoints with enhanced GetProcesses API
description:
Update the GetProcesses endpoint in ProcessEndpoints.cs to support the new filtering, sorting, and search functionality:

Current endpoint (line 99): Currently returns empty list
Target endpoint: GET /api/v1/processes with query parameters

Query Parameters to support:
- searchTerm (string, optional): Fuzzy search on process name
- connectionFilter (string, optional): 'any' | 'withConnections' | 'withoutConnections', default: 'any'
- sortBy (string, optional): 'name' | 'createdAt', default: 'createdAt'
- sortDirection (string, optional): 'asc' | 'desc', default: 'desc' 
- page (int, optional): Page number, default: 1
- limit (int, optional): Items per page, default: 20, max: 100

Response format:
- 200 OK with PagedResult<ProcessResponse>
- Include OpenAPI documentation for all parameters
- Add parameter validation (limit max 100, page >= 1)
- Map query parameters to GetProcessesQuery
- Send via IMediator to GetProcessesQueryHandler

Files to modify:
- src/backend/ProcessGraph.API/Endpoints/ProcessEndpoints.cs
- Update OpenAPI documentation with parameter descriptions

title: Add PostgreSQL query optimizations for process filtering
description:
Optimize PostgreSQL queries for the new GetProcesses functionality:

Database optimizations needed:
1. Ensure pg_trgm extension is enabled for fuzzy search
2. Verify GIN index exists on name field: IX_processes_name
3. Add index on created_at for sorting performance
4. Consider adding functional index for connection counting if needed

Query optimizations:
1. Use efficient JSONB queries to check for connections in graph data
2. Implement connection filter logic:
   - WithConnections: WHERE EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM jsonb_array_elements(graph->'nodes') AS node WHERE jsonb_array_length(node->'connections') > 0)
   - WithoutConnections: WHERE NOT EXISTS (similar query)
3. Use similarity() function from pg_trgm for better fuzzy search ranking
4. Combine similarity search with ILIKE for broader matching

Performance considerations:
- Test query performance with sample data
- Consider query plan analysis for optimization
- Ensure proper parameterized queries to prevent SQL injection

Files to modify:
- src/backend/ProcessGraph.Infrastructure/Repositories/ProcessRepository.cs
- Database migration files (if new indexes needed)
- Consider adding query performance tests

title: Register GetProcessesQuery in DI container
description:
Register the new GetProcessesQuery and GetProcessesQueryHandler in the dependency injection container:

1. Register GetProcessesQueryHandler in Application layer services
2. Ensure IQuery<PagedResult<ProcessResponse>> is properly registered
3. Verify MediatR can resolve the handler correctly
4. Add any missing service registrations for new models/enums

Files to modify:
- src/backend/ProcessGraph.Application/DependencyInjection/ServiceCollectionExtensions.cs
- src/backend/ProcessGraph.API/Program.cs (if needed)

Verification:
- Ensure the endpoint can resolve dependencies at startup
- Test that MediatR can find and execute the handler
- Verify all dependencies are available in the DI container

title: Integrate Mediator NuGet package into Application project
description:
Integrate the Mediator package from https://github.com/martinothamar/Mediator into the backend Application project. This includes:
- Adding the Mediator NuGet package dependency
- Configuring DI container to register handlers
- Setting up Mediator service registration  
- Updating routers to use Mediator for sending commands
- No tests required for initial integration
